title: The Compression Wound Archive
hypothesis: An archive where records exist at variable compression depths; interaction 'decompresses' them via vertical drag, causing text to become legible but structurally unstable, with each decompression step permanently reducing record coherence in localStorage
dataModel: seeded archive records with variable compression levels (3-9 stages per record), each containing fragment IDs, dates, witness names, and evidence codes stored as nested compression states with character-level integrity maps
recordCountStrategy: Seeded prime number generator: recordCount = nth prime where n = floor(seededRandom(seed) × 7.3) + 5, yielding counts between 11 and 59 records, never 18
interactionModel: mousedown initiates decompression drag on a record; vertical drag distance maps to decompression depth, progressively revealing compressed text while permanently corrupting random characters; mouseup locks current state to localStorage; dblclick attempts recompression which fails 40% of the time, leaving orphan text fragments that persist as floating SVG elements
stateModel: Per-record state: compressionDepth (0-9), characterIntegrity map (position → corruption 0.0-1.0), orphanFragments array, decompressionCount, lastAccessTimestamp. All persisted to localStorage under archive-seed key. Characters exceeding corruption threshold 0.7 are permanently lost and replaced with archival placeholder glyphs
visualLanguage: DOM cards with SVG overlay for corruption visualization; compressed records appear as narrow horizontal bands with monospace text partially visible through striated compression lines; decompressed records expand vertically revealing more text with increasing character corruption shown via SVG displacement filters and layered opacity; orphan fragments rendered as detached SVG text elements drifting slightly; palette: archive beige #f5f0e8, ink black #1a1a1a, corruption rust #c45a3c, placeholder glyph blue-gray #7a8b9a
expectedStructuralChange: Introduces vertical drag depth as continuous decompression axis rather than binary click states. First draft with character-level corruption tracking and partial reversibility failure mechanic. Variable record count per seed breaks fixed-array pattern. Decompression-as-reading metaphor extends degradation-on-access into a tactile, duration-based interaction where longer engagement causes deeper corruption
